nodes.description: A curated collection of spoken utterances that blend Nepali and English (often called Nepglish). The corpus provides audio recordings together with transcriptions and is essential for training language‑specific speech technologies.

Techniques for automatically identifying whether an utterance is Nepali, English, or a Nepglish mix. Accurate detection is a prerequisite for routing the audio to the appropriate acoustic and language models.

Automatic Speech Recognition models that transcribe spoken Nepali, English, and their code‑mixed forms. Training relies on the Nepglish speech corpus and benefits from prior language‑detection to select the correct model variant.
A full‑stack system that converts raw audio into textual output, integrating acoustic modeling, language modeling, and post‑processing. It builds directly on a trained Nepali‑Ne​pglish ASR component.

Dialogue management and natural language understanding components that enable interactive voice agents. It requires reliable transcription from the speech‑to‑text engine and knowledge of the spoken language via detection.

Banking services accessed through spoken interaction, such as balance inquiries, fund transfers, and transaction confirmations. Successful deployment depends on accurate speech‑to‑text conversion and robust conversational AI for handling banking intents.

Voice‑driven assistants that can operate across multiple languages, switching seamlessly between Nepali, English, and Nepglish. They rely on language detection to select the proper speech‑to‑text engine and on downstream dialogue capabilities.
